message:
Add simulate method support to Telegram and Twitter connectors

Implements handle_simulate() for both connectors to support FCP2 simulation
requests. The simulate method allows callers to check if an action would
be permitted without actually executing it.

Both implementations:
- Parse SimulateRequest from JSON params
- Return SimulateResponse::allowed(req.id) for all requests
- Wire up the "simulate" method in the main message handler

This is a baseline implementation that allows all simulations. Future
work may add connector-specific permission checks based on capability
grants or rate limits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -84,6 +84,7 @@ async fn handle_message(connector: &mut TelegramConnector, message: &str) -> ser
         "health" => connector.handle_health().await,
         "introspect" => connector.handle_introspect().await,
         "invoke" => connector.handle_invoke(params).await,
+        "simulate" => connector.handle_simulate(params).await,
         "subscribe" => connector.handle_subscribe(params).await,
         "shutdown" => connector.handle_shutdown(params).await,
         _ => Err(fcp_core::FcpError::InvalidRequest {
